description: Implement the complete MBG model codebase including all modules, models, and utilities. This is a single comprehensive feature covering the entire implementation since all components are interdependent.
steps: Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/requirements.txt with: torch>=2.0.0, mamba-ssm>=1.2.0 (optional), numpy>=1.24.0, scipy>=1.10.0, einops>=0.7.0, scikit-learn>=1.3.0

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/utils/electrode_positions.py - Define standard 10-20 system 2D electrode coordinates for 19-channel, 22-channel, and 64-channel montages. Include function get_electrode_positions(num_channels) that returns dict of name->coords, and compute_distance_matrix(positions) that returns NxN distance matrix.

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/utils/graph_utils.py - Implement: (1) bfs_ordering(adjacency_matrix, seed_idx) returning node visit order via BFS, (2) dfs_ordering(adjacency_matrix, seed_idx) returning node visit order via DFS, (3) gaussian_adjacency(distance_matrix, sigma) converting distances to adjacency weights via Gaussian kernel exp(-d^2 / (2*sigma^2))

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/modules/config_mamba.py - Dataclass MambaConfig with fields: d_model=200, d_intermediate=0, n_layer=12, ssm_cfg={}, attn_layer_idx=[], attn_cfg={}, rms_norm=True, residual_in_fp32=True, fused_add_norm=False. Follow pattern from /projects/sandbox/EEGMamba/modules/config_mamba.py but set fused_add_norm=False for CPU compatibility.

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/modules/mixer_seq_simple.py - Port from /projects/sandbox/EEGMamba/modules/mixer_seq_simple.py with these changes: (1) Add try/except for mamba_ssm imports with a fallback that defines a SimpleMambaFallback class using Linear+GELU+Linear as a placeholder mixer when mamba_ssm is unavailable, (2) When mamba_ssm is not available, create_block should use the fallback mixer, (3) Use nn.LayerNorm instead of RMSNorm when triton ops unavailable, (4) Set fused_add_norm=False path as default, (5) Keep the bidirectional flip logic in forward(). The MixerModel class signature and forward behavior must match EEGMamba's.
Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/models/__init__.py with imports: from .mbg import MBG

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/models/graph_construction.py - Implement AdaptiveAdjacency module: (1) __init__(num_channels, d_model, sigma=1.0) stores prior adjacency from electrode distances via Gaussian kernel, (2) learnable alpha parameter (nn.Parameter scalar initialized to 0.5), (3) forward(x) where x is (B*T, C, D): compute A_learned via attention (Q=W_q*x, K=W_k*x, A_learned = softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d))), combine A = alpha*A_prior + (1-alpha)*A_learned, return A. Include get_prior_adjacency(num_channels) function that uses electrode_positions to build the fixed prior.

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/models/graph_attention.py - Implement GraphAttentionLayer module: (1) __init__(d_model, nhead, dropout=0.1, num_channels=22) creates multi-head graph attention with AdaptiveAdjacency, (2) forward(x) where x is (B, C, T, D): reshape to (B*T, C, D), compute multi-head attention masked/weighted by adjacency matrix A, reshape back to (B, C, T, D). Implement GAT from scratch with pure PyTorch (no PyG): for each head, compute attention coefficients e_ij = LeakyReLU(a^T [Wh_i || Wh_j]), mask with adjacency, softmax, aggregate.

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/models/gem_block.py - Implement GEMBlock module: (1) __init__(d_model, nhead, dim_feedforward, n_layer_mamba=2, num_channels=22, dropout=0.1), (2) Contains: norm1 (LayerNorm), graph_attention (GraphAttentionLayer), norm2 (LayerNorm), mamba_temporal (MixerModel with n_layer=n_layer_mamba for per-channel temporal processing), fusion_gate (Linear(2*d_model, d_model) + sigmoid), norm3 (LayerNorm), ffn (Linear->GELU->Dropout->Linear->Dropout), (3) forward(x) where x is (B,C,T,D): z=norm1(x), z_spatial=graph_attention(z), z_temporal via mamba on reshape (B*C,T,D) then back to (B,C,T,D), gate=sigmoid(W_g*cat(z_spatial,z_temporal)), fused=gate*z_spatial+(1-gate)*z_temporal, x=x+fused, x=x+ffn(norm3(x)), return x

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/models/mbg.py - Main MBG model class: (1) __init__(in_dim=200, out_dim=200, d_model=200, dim_feedforward=800, seq_len=30, n_layer=12, nhead=8, num_channels=22) - SAME signature style as CBraMod/EEGMamba, (2) patch_embedding (PatchEmbedding class same as EEGMamba's pattern: temporal Conv2d + spectral FFT projection + positional Conv2d), (3) encoder: nn.ModuleList of n_layer//2 GEM blocks (since each GEM block contains internal mamba layers), or simpler: use n_layer GEM blocks each with n_layer_mamba=1, (4) proj_out: Linear(d_model, out_dim), (5) forward(x, mask=None): patch_emb->stacked GEM blocks->proj_out, (6) _weights_init function, (7) __main__ block that creates model and runs forward pass with shape (2, 22, 4, 200) on CPU, prints output shape

Create /projects/sandbox/mbg/models/model_for_tuab.py - Follow exact pattern from CBraMod/EEGMamba: Model class with __init__(param) that creates MBG backbone, optionally loads pretrained weights, replaces proj_out with Identity, adds classifier head with same options (avgpooling_patch_reps, all_patch_reps_onelayer, all_patch_reps_twolayer, all_patch_reps)
acceptance_criteria: Running `cd /projects/sandbox/mbg && python3 models/mbg.py` executes successfully and prints output shape (2, 22, 4, 200)

All Python files are importable without errors: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); from models.mbg import MBG; from models.graph_attention import GraphAttentionLayer; from models.gem_block import GEMBlock"
The MBG model signature matches CBraMod/EEGMamba: MBG(in_dim=200, out_dim=200, d_model=200, dim_feedforward=800, seq_len=30, n_layer=12, nhead=8)
Input/output dimensions are correct: input (B, C, T, P) -> output (B, C, T, out_dim)
No dependency on torch_geometric or any external graph library
mamba-ssm import has graceful fallback when library is not available
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/mbg && python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); from models import MBG; from models.graph_attention import GraphAttentionLayer; from models.gem_block import GEMBlock; from models.graph_construction import AdaptiveAdjacency; from utils.electrode_positions import get_electrode_positions; from utils.graph_utils import bfs_ordering, dfs_ordering; print('All imports OK')"

cd /projects/sandbox/mbg && python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); import torch; from models.mbg import MBG; m=MBG(in_dim=200,out_dim=200,d_model=200,dim_feedforward=800,seq_len=30,n_layer=4,nhead=8,num_channels=22); x=torch.randn(2,22,4,200); y=m(x); assert y.shape==(2,22,4,200), f'Expected (2,22,4,200) got {y.shape}'; print('Shape test PASSED')"
findings: All verification tests pass on CPU. Mamba fallback uses Linear+GELU+Linear as a placeholder mixer when mamba-ssm is unavailable. The model uses n_layer GEM blocks each with n_layer_mamba=1 internally. PatchEmbedding uses Conv2d temporal projection (kernel 49, stride 25) producing 25 channels mapped to d_model=200 via view, combined with FFT spectral projection. The 64-channel electrode map has exactly 64 positions from the 10-10 system.
